distribution_scope: tracked repository source for which the project has authority to grant Apache-2.0 rights
documentation and configuration under the repository license
deterministic source-candidate archives produced from an exact Git commit
reasoning: The owner explicitly requested that RepoGround remain open source and free to use.
Apache-2.0 permits use, modification, and redistribution and provides an explicit patent grant.
The source-candidate boundary references dependencies but does not embed installed third-party packages.
Project-name stewardship is separated from software copyright permissions through TRADEMARK_POLICY.md.
The repository-level decision can grant only rights held by or validly licensed to the relevant copyright holder.

supersedes.reason: The documented reopen condition was satisfied by the explicit owner instruction on 2026-07-18.

does_not_establish: trademark registration or global name clearance
authority to relicense independently copyrighted historical contributions without any consent legally required
permission to misrepresent modified builds as official RepoGround releases
clearance for bundled third-party binaries, containers, model weights, or datasets
current_license_notice: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, subject to the rights-authority boundary documented in docs/release/licensing.md.
